title: Environment Setup & Dart Basics
topics: Install Flutter SDK, Dart SDK, FVM (Flutter Version Manager)
Set up Android Studio/VS Code, Flutter/Dart plugins, an Android emulator and iOS simulator
Dart syntax: variables, data types (`int`, `double`, `String`, `bool`), `var`/`final`/`const`
Null safety basics: `?`, `!`, `late`
practice: Write 5 tiny Dart scripts in DartPad (temperature converter, simple calculator, etc.)

title: Control Flow & Functions
topics: `if/else`, `switch`, ternary operator
Loops: `for`, `for-in`, `while`, `do-while`
Functions: positional params, named params, optional params, default values, arrow functions
practice: FizzBuzz, a grade calculator, a simple number-guessing CLI game

title: OOP Basics
topics: Classes, constructors, named constructors, factory constructors
Fields, getters/setters, `this` keyword
practice: Model a `BankAccount` class with deposit/withdraw logic and validation

title: OOP Advanced
topics: Inheritance (`extends`), method overriding, `super`
Abstract classes, interfaces (`implements`), mixins (`with`)
Enums (including enhanced enums with methods)
practice: Build a `Shape` hierarchy (Circle, Square, Triangle) with polymorphic `area()` calculation

title: Collections & Generics
topics: `List`, `Set`, `Map` — creation, iteration, common methods
Higher-order functions: `map`, `where`, `reduce`, `fold`, `forEach`
Generics basics (`List<T>`, generic classes/functions)
practice: Given a list of "student" objects, filter, sort, and aggregate data (average score, top scorer, etc.)

title: Error Handling & Null Safety Deep Dive
topics: `try/catch/finally`, `throw`, custom exception classes
Null-aware operators: `??`, `??=`, `?.`, `!`
Sound null safety patterns and common pitfalls
practice: Build an input validator that handles bad data gracefully with custom exceptions

title: Asynchronous Dart I
topics: `Future`, `async`/`await`, `Future.wait`, `Future.delayed`
Error handling in async code (`try/catch` with `await`)
practice: Simulate 3 "API calls" with artificial delay and combine their results

title: Asynchronous Dart II
topics: `Stream`, `StreamController`, single-subscription vs broadcast streams
`async*` generators, `yield`
practice: Build a simple event stream (e.g., a simulated stock price ticker) and listen to it

title: Dart Tooling & Packages
topics: `pubspec.yaml`, pub.dev, adding/managing dependencies
Dart formatter, `analysis_options.yaml`, linting basics
Organizing code into libraries/files, `import`/`export`
practice: Split your Day 5–8 code into a small reusable Dart package with proper file structure

title: Revision Project 1: Console Student Management System
topics: Combine everything from Days 1–9: OOP (student/course classes), collections (enrollment lists, grade aggregation), async (simulated data fetch/save), error handling (invalid input), and clean file organization
This is a pure Dart (no Flutter UI) console app — the goal is to prove Dart fluency before touching widgets

title: Flutter Basics & the Widget Tree
topics: What Flutter is, how the widget tree/Element tree works (conceptually)
`StatelessWidget` vs `StatefulWidget`, `build()` method, `setState()` intro
Hot reload vs hot restart, project folder structure
practice: Create a new Flutter project, run it on both an emulator and simulator

title: Basic Widgets
topics: `Text`, `Image`, `Icon`, `Container`, `SizedBox`, `Padding`
Widget properties and how styling props work
practice: Build a static profile card (avatar, name, bio, icons)

title: Layout Widgets I
topics: `Row`, `Column`, `Stack`, `Positioned`, `Align`
`MainAxisAlignment`, `CrossAxisAlignment`, `MainAxisSize`
practice: Recreate a real app's home screen layout using only these widgets

title: Layout Widgets II
topics: `ListView`, `GridView`, `Expanded`, `Flexible`, `Wrap`
Scrollable content, `ListView.builder` for dynamic lists
practice: Build a scrollable product/article list screen with mock data

title: Styling & Theming
topics: `ThemeData`, `ColorScheme`, `TextTheme`, Material 3 basics
Cupertino widgets for iOS-native feel
Light/dark mode support
practice: Apply one consistent theme (with dark mode) across all screens built so far

title: User Input & Forms
topics: `TextField`, `TextEditingController`, `Form`, `TextFormField`, validators
Buttons: `ElevatedButton`, `TextButton`, `OutlinedButton`, `IconButton`
practice: Build a login/signup form UI with client-side validation (no backend yet)

title: Navigation & Routing
topics: `Navigator.push`/`pop`, named routes
`go_router` setup: declarative routing, route params, deep links
practice: Wire up navigation across 4–5 screens with data passed between them

title: Gestures & Interactivity
topics: `GestureDetector`, `InkWell`, tap/long-press/drag/swipe detection
Dismissible widgets, pull-to-refresh (`RefreshIndicator`)
practice: Add swipe-to-delete and pull-to-refresh to your Day 14 list screen

title: Assets, Fonts & Responsive Design
topics: Managing images/fonts via `pubspec.yaml`
`MediaQuery`, `LayoutBuilder`, responsive breakpoints
practice: Make one screen adapt cleanly between phone and tablet width

title: Revision Project 2: Static Multi-Screen App
topics: Build a fully static (no state management library, no backend) multi-screen app — e.g., a recipe browser or a mini portfolio app
Must include: custom theming with dark mode, forms with validation, `go_router` navigation, gestures, and responsive layout
This proves UI fluency before architecture is introduced

title: State Management Fundamentals
topics: `setState` deep dive: what triggers rebuilds, widget rebuild mechanics
Lifting state up, when `setState` stops being enough
practice: Build an interactive quiz/counter app using only `setState`, then identify its scaling limits

title: Riverpod Basics
topics: `ProviderScope`, `Provider`, `StateProvider`, `ref.watch` vs `ref.read`
Why Riverpod over `setState`/`Provider` at scale (compile-time safety, no `BuildContext` coupling)
practice: Convert your Day 21 app from `setState` to Riverpod

title: Riverpod Advanced
topics: `AsyncNotifier`/`AsyncValue` for async state (loading/data/error built-in)
Provider `family` and dependent providers
practice: Build a screen driven by simulated async data using `AsyncNotifier`

title: App Architecture I: MVVM
topics: Flutter's official architecture pattern: View → ViewModel → Repository → Service
Feature-first folder structure (`lib/features/<feature>/...`)
practice: Restructure your Day 20 project into this MVVM/feature-first layout

title: App Architecture II: Clean Layering
topics: Domain/data/presentation separation, use-case pattern (when it's worth the extra layer)
Dependency inversion: why ViewModels depend on abstractions, not concrete services
practice: Add a `Repository` abstraction (interface) with a fake in-memory implementation

title: Data Modeling with Freezed
topics: Immutable data classes with `freezed`, `json_serializable`
Sealed/union types for UI state (`Loading`/`Success`/`Error`)
`build_runner` code generation workflow
practice: Model your app's core data classes and a `UiState` union type

title: Local Storage I: Key-Value
topics: `shared_preferences` for simple settings/flags
When key-value storage is (and isn't) enough
practice: Persist user preferences (theme choice, onboarding-seen flag)

title: Local Storage II: Structured Data
topics: `Drift` (or `sqflite`) for relational local data, schema design, migrations
practice: Build a CRUD "notes" feature backed by Drift

title: Dependency Injection & Wiring
topics: Using Riverpod providers as your DI mechanism
Provider overrides for testing (swap real repository for a fake)
practice: Wire Service → Repository → ViewModel → View end-to-end for one feature

title: Revision Project 3: Full-Architecture To-Do App
topics: Build a complete to-do list app using everything from Days 21–29: Riverpod state management, MVVM/feature-first structure, Drift persistence, freezed models, form input, navigation
This is your first "real" architected app — treat it like production code

title: Networking I: REST Basics
topics: HTTP methods, status codes, JSON parsing
`http` package basics: GET/POST requests
practice: Fetch data from a public API and render it in a list

title: Networking II: Dio & Robust Clients
topics: `Dio` setup, interceptors (logging, auth headers), timeouts, retry logic
Centralized error handling for network failures
practice: Rebuild Day 31's API call with Dio, add retry-on-failure logic

title: Repository Pattern: Local + Remote
topics: Cache-then-network strategy, offline-first thinking
Merging Drift (local) and Dio (remote) behind one repository interface
practice: Add offline caching to your Day 31–32 API-driven screen

title: Authentication Basics
topics: Token-based auth flow (login → store token → attach to requests)
`flutter_secure_storage` for sensitive data (tokens, credentials)
practice: Build a login/signup flow with secure token storage

title: Firebase Integration
topics: Firebase project setup for both Android and iOS
Firestore basics: reads/writes/streams
practice: Sync one feature's data (e.g., notes or tasks) to Firestore in real time

title: Notifications & Background Work
topics: `flutter_local_notifications`: scheduling, permissions
`workmanager` for Android background tasks; understanding iOS background execution limits
practice: Add a reminder/notification feature to an existing screen

title: Animations I: Implicit
topics: `AnimatedContainer`, `AnimatedOpacity`, `AnimatedSwitcher`
When implicit animation is enough vs when you need explicit control
practice: Animate state transitions (expanding cards, fade-in lists)

title: Animations II: Explicit & Hero
topics: `AnimationController`, `Tween`, `CurvedAnimation`
`Hero` widget for shared-element transitions between screens
practice: Add a Hero transition from a list item to its detail screen

title: UX Polish: Loading, Error & Empty States
topics: Global error handling patterns, consistent loading/error/empty widgets
Skeleton loaders vs spinners, user-friendly error messages
practice: Add polished loading/error/empty states across all major screens built so far

title: Revision Project 4: Flagship App Part 1 — Student Productivity App (Core Build)
topics: Start your capstone: a **student productivity app** combining a to-do list, a Pomodoro-style study timer, and an expense tracker
Build the to-do and timer modules using everything so far: Riverpod, MVVM architecture, Drift persistence, notifications (timer alerts), animations, and polished loading/error states
This is the app you'll finish, test, and publish in the phases ahead

title: Expense Tracker Module & Data Visualization
topics: Modeling financial data (categories, amounts, dates)
Charts with `fl_chart` (bar/line/pie for spending breakdown)
practice: Build the expense tracker module with a visual spending summary

title: On-Device AI Integration
topics: Firebase AI Logic (Gemini) or Genkit for Dart — calling an AI model from Flutter
Practical use case: auto-categorizing expenses or summarizing study notes
practice: Add one AI-powered feature to your flagship app — your strongest resume differentiator

title: Testing I: Unit Tests
topics: `test` package fundamentals, `mocktail` for mocking dependencies
Testing ViewModels and repositories in isolation
practice: Write unit tests for your flagship app's core business logic

title: Testing II: Widget & Golden Tests
topics: `flutter_test`: `pump()`, `pumpAndSettle()`, finding widgets, simulating taps
Golden (screenshot) test basics
practice: Write widget tests for 2–3 key screens

title: Testing III: Integration Tests
topics: `integration_test` package for full end-to-end flows
Testing across real navigation and state changes
practice: Write one E2E test covering a critical user journey (e.g., add task → complete task)

title: Performance Optimization
topics: Flutter DevTools: widget rebuild profiling, frame rendering stats
`const` constructors, avoiding unnecessary rebuilds, list virtualization
practice: Profile your flagship app and fix at least 2 real performance issues

title: Responsive & Adaptive Design Deep Dive
topics: Supporting tablets, foldables, and varying orientations properly
Adaptive layouts vs simply "responsive" ones
practice: Make your flagship app usable and clean on a tablet-sized screen

title: Accessibility
topics: `Semantics` widget, screen reader support, minimum tap target sizes, color contrast
practice: Run an accessibility audit on your flagship app and fix the issues found

title: CI/CD Basics
topics: GitHub Actions: running `flutter analyze` and `flutter test` automatically on push
Basic build automation concepts
practice: Set up a CI pipeline that fails the build on lint/test errors

title: Revision Project 5: Flagship App Part 2 — Feature-Complete & Fully Tested
topics: Finish integrating all three modules (to-do, timer, expense tracker) into one cohesive app
Ensure unit + widget + integration tests all pass, CI is green, performance and accessibility issues are fixed
This is now a genuinely production-quality app

title: Build Flavors & Environment Config
topics: Dev/staging/prod flavors, managing environment-specific config (API URLs, keys)
practice: Set up flavors for your flagship app

title: App Signing & Release Builds
topics: Android: keystore generation and signing config
iOS: certificates, provisioning profiles, Xcode signing setup
practice: Generate a signed release build for both Android and iOS

title: Store Presence I: Google Play
topics: Play Console account setup, store listing, screenshots, Data Safety form
practice: Prepare and fill out your Play Store listing

title: Store Presence II: Apple App Store
topics: Apple Developer account, App Store Connect, App Privacy details
practice: Prepare and fill out your App Store listing

title: Analytics & Crash Reporting
topics: Firebase Analytics (key event tracking) and Crashlytics integration
practice: Add analytics events to your app's core user flows

title: Beta Testing
topics: Play Console internal testing track, Apple TestFlight
Collecting and triaging real user feedback
practice: Distribute your app to a small group of testers and log their feedback

title: Monetization Basics
topics: Freemium models, `in_app_purchase` package, ARPU as a concept
practice: Implement one basic premium-feature gate in your app

title: Portfolio & Documentation
topics: Writing a strong technical README, architecture diagrams, decision rationale
Adding the project as a case study to your portfolio site
practice: Finish the README and add this app's case study to your portfolio

title: Interview Preparation
topics: Common Dart/Flutter interview questions (Riverpod internals, widget rebuild mechanics, `Future` vs `Stream`)
Mobile system design basics: offline-first sync, pagination, image caching
practice: Do a self-run mock interview — write out answers to 10 architecture questions cold, then check them

title: Final Project: Launch Day
topics: Publish the app to the Play Store (production track) and submit it to the App Store for review
Write a launch post documenting the build: architecture choices, the AI feature, and lessons learned
Do an honest gap audit: list what you still don't know (see below) as your next 6–18 month roadmap
**Scale experience:** large multi-package Dart/Flutter monorepos, large-team git workflows, code review culture
**Platform channels:** writing native Kotlin/Swift bridges when a plugin doesn't exist for something you need
**Deep testing:** CI device farms, flaky-test debugging, contract testing against real backends
**Security:** certificate pinning, code obfuscation (`--obfuscate`), secure key management
**Breadth:** Flutter Web, Desktop (Windows/macOS), and knowing when *not* to use Flutter
**Leadership signals:** mentoring, writing architecture decision records (ADRs), owning a codebase's technical direction
**Daily:** one focused build session + 15 minutes reading the Flutter blog/release notes
**Weekly:** commit progress publicly (GitHub streak + one LinkedIn/X post about what you built)
**Bi-weekly:** revisit and refactor old code with newly learned patterns — this is exactly how "junior code" becomes "senior code"
[Flutter official docs](https://docs.flutter.dev)
[Flutter's official app architecture guide](https://docs.flutter.dev/app-architecture) (the MVVM + feature-first pattern this roadmap follows)
[Riverpod docs](https://riverpod.dev)
[Dart language tour](https://dart.dev/language)
Google's official Flutter samples repo — study real architecture directly
Play Console Academy + App Store Connect documentation for publishing/monetization
